attributes.description: Alerts when a single client IP triggers a large number of attack-category WAF rule matches (SQLi, XSS, RCE, LFI, RFI, PHP injection, Java injection) over a 15-minute window. Works in both Detection and Prevention mode — does not depend on Blocked actions.
attributes.name: [Azure Application Gateway OTel] WAF attack concentration from client IP

investigation_guide.blob:
## WAF Attack Concentration From Client IP

A single client IP triggered a large number of WAF rule matches in attack categories (SQLi, XSS, RCE, LFI, RFI, PHP/Java injection). The attack signal is independent of WAF mode: in Detection mode the request still reaches the backend; in Prevention mode it is blocked.

### Triage Steps
1. Open the WAF dashboard and filter to the offending client IP.
2. Review the rule-group distribution for this IP — a focused attack will be concentrated in one or two categories.
3. Check the user agent (`attributes.user_agent.original`) and target paths (`attributes.url.path`) for known scanner signatures.
4. Determine if the requests were blocked (Prevention) or only logged (Detection):
   `FROM logs-azure.application_gateway.otel-default | WHERE attributes.azure.category == "ApplicationGatewayFirewallLog" AND attributes.client.address == "<ip>" | STATS c = COUNT(*) BY attributes.security_rule.action, attributes.security_rule.ruleset.mode`
5. If WAF is in Detection mode and the activity is hostile, consider switching the policy to Prevention or adding an IP-based custom block rule.

### Tuning
- The 20-match floor avoids paging on background scanner noise. Raise it for noisy public endpoints, or lower it for sensitive applications.
- Add allow-listed scanner IPs (security team probes) by extending the WHERE clause with `AND attributes.client.address NOT IN (...)`.
- Extend the attack-category list to include `RFI`, `WEB-SHELL`, `FIX-SESSION` etc. as needed.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM logs-azure.application_gateway.otel-default
| WHERE attributes.azure.category == "ApplicationGatewayFirewallLog"
  AND attributes.client.address IS NOT NULL
// Flag rule matches that map to high-signal attack categories.
// security_rule.ruleset.name encodes the rule group, e.g. REQUEST-942-APPLICATION-ATTACK-SQLI.
// Extend this list to suit your environment.
| EVAL is_attack = CASE(
    attributes.security_rule.ruleset.name LIKE "*SQLI*"
      OR attributes.security_rule.ruleset.name LIKE "*XSS*"
      OR attributes.security_rule.ruleset.name LIKE "*RCE*"
      OR attributes.security_rule.ruleset.name LIKE "*LFI*"
      OR attributes.security_rule.ruleset.name LIKE "*RFI*"
      OR attributes.security_rule.ruleset.name LIKE "*PHP*"
      OR attributes.security_rule.ruleset.name LIKE "*JAVA*",
    1, 0)
| STATS
    attack_matches = SUM(is_attack),
    total_matches = COUNT(*)
  BY attributes.client.address,
     resource.attributes.cloud.resource_id
// Tune this threshold up for noisy public endpoints, down for sensitive ones.
| WHERE attack_matches > 20
| SORT attack_matches DESC
| LIMIT 10
